rows.description: Computus Joannis Longbeare Rectoris a die Novembris 1730 ad eundem diem revolutum 1731
rows.notes: Top title is partly faded and some letters (surname and a few Latin words) are abbreviated; I reconstructed the Latin phrase from context and common ledger phrasing ('Computus ... a die Novembris 1730 ad eundem diem revolutum 1731'). Characters are legible but not crystal-clear.
Centered section heading clearly written. Letters are legible and there are no amounts on this row.
Amounts (10 0 0) are very clear and aligned to the three ruling columns; description short but legible.
Pounds and shillings are clear (26, 12). Pence reads as '9' with a small loop/mark after it interpreted as the halfpenny 'ob' symbol (so 9½d). The loop is faint which reduces confidence slightly.
Amount (40 0 0) aligns well with the pounds/shillings/pence columns and is clear. The place name is slightly ambiguous in spelling but readable enough.
Large pound figure (145 0 0) is unambiguous and aligns exactly with the pounds column; 'South Newington Rect.' is clearly written.
Amount (20 0 0) clear and well aligned. The place name appears as 'Eddington' (or similar); spelling is typical variant but readable.
All three monetary columns read clearly as 14 pounds, 8 shillings, 10 pence. Description 'Chase-hill' is legible.
Amount (2 0 0) is clear. Name reads as 'Lucas Eaton' with good legibility.
Monetary columns read as 14, 3, 3. Description appears to be 'The Quit Rents' (handwriting slightly compressed), so reading is moderately certain.
Amounts 0 7 6 are clearly aligned. The description is longer but readable as 'Ralph Marshals Capons'; spacing and capitals support this reading.
Entry reads 10 pounds, 9 shillings, 11 pence; all three digits are clear and within valid ranges. Description 'Bigg' is short and distinct.
Amounts 2, 19, 5 align with columns. Shillings read as 19 (close to the 20 limit but valid). The phrase 'Caleb alias Lane' is legible though the middle word is slightly cramped.
Amount 0 15 0 is clear and centered in the monetary columns. 'Bannister' reads cleanly.
Amount (1 0 0) unambiguous. Description reads 'Leaden-Hall' (hyphenation and capitals visible).
Monetary columns clearly show 4, 12, 11. 'Sadler' is legible; small flourishes do not affect numeric reading.
Amount (1 13 4) is clear. 'Hart Hall' reads well; spacing between words is distinct.

Pounds and shillings (4,1) are clear. Pence looks like '8' with a small trailing loop interpreted as the halfpenny mark (8½d). The fractional loop is faint and slightly ambiguous, hence reduced confidence.

Numeric columns read as 4, 10, 4. The place/name looks like 'Clifton Ferry' (handwritten 'Ferry' could be 'Perry' in another reading) — chosen 'Ferry' because the initial letter has a descender matching 'F'.
Pounds and shillings (7,9) are clear. Pence appears as 11 with a small halfpenny loop after it -> interpreted as 11½d. The fractional mark is visible but not perfectly sharp.
Amount (6 0 0) readable and well aligned. The name looks like 'Tretheven' or similar Cornish/Devon variant; spelling uncertain but entry is legible.

Monetary columns read 26,10,0 clearly. The description names several parties in a list; handwriting is a little compressed making exact spellings ('Sadlane'/'Sadlanee', 'Sparkes', 'Meriton') moderately uncertain.
Numbers 6,13,4 are aligned and clear. Place name reads as 'Medenham' though the first vowel could be slightly ambiguous; context suggests this reading.
Final visible entry shows 4 0 0 clearly. The description 'Back Gate, Boyse' (or 'Boyse') is legible; surname spelling is reasonably certain.
